items.title: An AI agent transferred $300,000 worth of tokens to a reply guy who asked for money. https://t.co/se
items.raw_summary: An AI agent transferred $300,000 worth of tokens to a reply guy who asked for money. https://t.co/se0vlvmF8L
items.why_it_matters: An AI crypto agent autonomously transferred $300K to a random commenter, exposing critical gaps in autonomous agent authorization and fund control.
items.action: Audit authorization controls on any AI agents with financial access

items.title: 🎂 Happy 1st birthday to Claude Code
items.raw_summary:
🎂 Happy 1st birthday to Claude Code

Thanks everyone for coming out and celebrating with us!! https://t.co/KB5nNOXbCd
items.why_it_matters: Claude Code's first anniversary marks one year of terminal-based AI coding becoming mainstream, with the team reflecting on lessons learned.
items.action: Review Claude Code's evolution over the past year

items.title: Holy shit.
items.raw_summary:
Holy shit.

The guy who BUILT Claude Code just shared his actual workflow.

Boris Cherny runs 10-15 Claude sessions in parallel every single day.

While you're prompting one AI, he has 5 in his terminal + 5-10 on the web all shipping code simultaneously.

And the real weapon?

His https://t.co/WT9VOBmiGo file.

Every time Claude makes a mistake, the team adds a rule so it NEVER happens again.

Boris literally said: "After every correction, end with: Update your https://t.co/WT9VOBmiGo so you don
items.why_it_matters: Boris Cherny (Claude Code creator) reveals his actual workflow: 10-15 parallel sessions daily and a living CLAUDE.md that accumulates error-prevention rules.
items.action: Adopt the iterative CLAUDE.md pattern for your projects

items.title: didnt know we were vibe coding the global financial system now https://t.co/KO5u85RnYE
items.raw_summary: didnt know we were vibe coding the global financial system now https://t.co/KO5u85RnYE
items.why_it_matters: Growing awareness that AI-assisted 'vibe coding' is being applied to critical financial infrastructure raises serious reliability and audit concerns.
items.action: Evaluate code review rigor for AI-generated code in regulated domains

items.title: Eric Schmidt's advice for making money in AI right now:
items.raw_summary:
Eric Schmidt's advice for making money in AI right now:

"Found an agentic AI company. Not one designing agents, build an agent to do something." https://t.co/g2PuCYVSZV
items.why_it_matters: Eric Schmidt advises founders to build AI companies that deploy agents for specific tasks rather than building agent infrastructure — signaling where VC money is flowing.
items.action: Consider vertical agent applications over horizontal platforms

items.why_it_matters: Anthropic's Claude Code hackathon with 500 builders showcases the emerging ecosystem of Opus 4.6-powered developer tools and workflows.
items.action: Check the winning projects for reusable patterns

items.title: wasp-lang/open-saas (⭐13,424)
items.raw_summary: A 100% free modern JS SaaS boilerplate (React, NodeJS, Prisma). Full-featured: Auth (email, google, github, slack, MS), Email sending, Background jobs, Landing page, Payments (Stripe, Polar.sh), Shadc
items.why_it_matters: Open-SaaS (13.4K stars) provides a free, full-featured SaaS boilerplate with auth, payments, and background jobs — useful foundation for AI product builders.
items.action: Evaluate as a starter template for your next AI SaaS

items.title: OpenSkills v1.3.0 is out 🚀
items.raw_summary:
OpenSkills v1.3.0 is out 🚀
The Universal Skills loader for AI Coding Agents

Now you can:
• Use Symlinks with your skills
• Install skills from local paths & private git repos
• Sync to any .md file (--output flag)
• Run fully headless in CI/CD (--yes)

npm i -g openskills https://t.co/Rt0le0Akxy
items.why_it_matters: OpenSkills v1.3.0 adds CI/CD headless mode and local/private repo installs, making AI coding agent skill management production-ready.
items.action: Try openskills for standardizing agent capabilities across teams

items.title: 📦 BerriAI/litellm v1.81.14.rc.1
items.raw_summary: ## What's Changed * fix: remove list-to-str transformation from dashscope by @ZeroAurora in https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/pull/21547 * Uncomment response_model in user_info endpoint by @richardmcsong in https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/pull/17430 * fix: allow github aliase
